item.title: French, German Governments Agree to Hold Equal Stakes in KNDS
item.description: The German and French governments are set to hold equal stakes in KNDS NV, one of Europe’s most important defense companies, as it gears up for an initial public offering in the near future.

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Intensifies With France on Red Alert
item.description: A heat wave gripping western Europe is forecast to strengthen this week, with days of dangerously hot and humid conditions and record-breaking temperatures in France, the UK, Spain and Germany.

item.title: Grace Shao on What the World Should Know About Chinese AI
item.description: This is how the Chinese ecosystem is different.

item.title: Odd Lots: Grace Shao on How Chinese AI is Different (Podcast)
item.description: China’s AI industry has changed a lot since DeepSeek released its cheap frontier model last year, and briefly sent US tech stocks falling. After being locked out of the most advanced chips, Chinese companies are now allowed to buy some Nvidia H200s. In fact, many of the big Chinese tech companies — like Baidu — are making a push to become full-stack players, with their own chips, models, and cloud infrastructure. Today’s guest is Grace Shao, an independent AI researcher and the author of the AI

item.title: Escrivá Says ECB Must Be Vigilant on Oil Price Impact on Wages
item.description: The European Central Bank must watch for any knock-on impact on wages from higher oil and commodity prices resulting from the Middle East conflict, Governing Council member Jose Luis Escrivá said.

item.title: Chinese Backing Helps African Startup Spiro Near Unicorn Status
item.description: Chinese investor NewTrails Capital will invest $55 million into African electric mobility startup Spiro, helping boost the company’s valuation.

item.title: Southern Water Gets £300m Equity as Asterion Joins Macquarie
item.description: Southern Water Ltd. reached a target to raise £1.2 billion ($1.6 billion) in equity to fund major network upgrades after securing the final tranche of investment from a new shareholder.

item.title: European Gas Edges Higher After Rocky Start to Iran Peace Talks
item.description: European natural gas edged higher after talks between the US and Iran appeared to get off to a shaky start, with President Donald Trump issuing fresh threats even as discussions on a peace deal proceed.

item.title: Bond Traders Finally Get Single View of Activity in UK Market
item.description: Bond investors gained access to a single stream of UK trading data after a transparency initiative that’s been years in the making went live.

item.title: Chinese Steel Market Enters Long Plateau After Property Crash
item.description: The Chinese steel market is navigating a prolonged weakening in demand rather than a cliff-drop collapse, as the steep decline from property is increasingly offset by manufacturing and exports, according to experts at an industry conference last week.

item.title: Peer-Beating Tech Fund Doubles China Exposure in Contrarian Bet
item.description: A peer-beating technology stock fund is taking profits on some hot US trades and adding exposure in China, seeing opportunities in the Asian nation’s internet giants and hardware makers.

item.title: Iranian Crude Prices Slashed as More Shipments Exit Hormuz
item.description: Sellers of Iranian crude to China have slashed prices after the Islamic Republic started shipping out millions of barrels following the interim peace deal that with the US.

item.title: EasyJet Rejects Three Separate Overtures From Castlelake
item.description: EasyJet Plc has rejected three separate offers from Castlelake LP, prompting the investment firm to take its latest proposal that values the budget carrier at about £4.74 billion ($6.3 billion) directly to shareholders.

item.title: Iranian Crude Exports Surge Via Hormuz as Activity Picks Up
item.description: Iran has ramped up the amount of oil it sends openly through the Strait of Hormuz to the highest since the war began, as shipping activity picks up while Tehran and Washington seek a lasting peace deal.

item.title: Korea Markets Chief Regrets Allowing Single-Stock Leveraged ETFs
item.description: South Korea’s top financial regulator said he regrets not blocking the launch of leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking single stocks, warning their negative side effects have grown significantly.

item.title: Carlyle Rethinks Portfolio Risk to Give Weather Insurance a Bigger Role
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. is unveiling a new framework for portfolio risk so that asset values reflect the insurance implications associated with severe weather shocks.

item.title: Pound Falls on Growing Political Upheaval
Philippines Slashes 2026 Growth Outlook on Iran War, Graft Mess
item.description: The Philippines cut its economic growth target for this year, as the country wrestled with the impact of costlier oil spurred by the Iran war and a government corruption crackdown.

item.title: Chinese AI Stocks Rally on Demand Optimism and Policy Support
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence-related stocks jumped, with sentiment boosted by a more supportive policy tone from Beijing and ongoing global demand for the technology.

item.title: Thai Shipper Remains Wary of Hormuz Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Bangkok-based Precious Shipping Pcl, whose vessel was struck in March by Iranian projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, said it remains cautious about sending ships through one of the world’s most important energy and trade chokepoints.

item.title: Trump Builds a New US Tariff Wall in Shakeup of Winners, Losers
item.description: President Donald Trump is rolling out new tools with the same protectionist goals after the Supreme Court ruled his sweeping global tariffs to be illegal. His administration wants the rebuilt wall of import taxes to mirror those Trump put on every major trading partner at the beginning of his second term.

item.title: Global Banks Drive India Asset-Backed Securities Sales to Record
item.description: India’s asset-backed securities market has surged to a record as global banks ramp up purchases to gain exposure to one of the world’s fastest-growing major economies.

item.title: Tencent Tests AI Assistant for Its Super App WeChat in China
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. has started testing a new AI assistant for WeChat as part of its efforts to catch up with peers in China’s white-hot artificial intelligence race.

item.title: Iran War Puts $124 Billion Gulf Remittance Lifeline at Risk
item.description: The conflict is putting billions of dollars in remittances at risk and testing a Gulf economic model built on foreign labor.

item.title: Kuwait Adds to Signs Hormuz Is Reopening With Offer for Products
item.description: Kuwait is asking customers to pick up refined petroleum from its ports deep inside the Persian Gulf, as the region’s oil giants try to ratchet up production and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increases.

item.title: Japan Insurers Sold Domestic Super-Long Bonds as Yields Soared
item.description: Japan’s insurers sold domestic super-long government bonds in May, reversing course from the start of the fiscal year as yields climbed to multi-decade highs.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mark Fresh High as US-Iran Talks Progress, Oil Falls
item.description: Emerging-market stocks rose to a fresh record as the US and Iran agreed upon a roadmap to a final peace deal, indicating progress in talks that have proceeded in fits and starts amid threats by President Donald Trump.

item.title: Asset Manager CSOP Lifts Options Cap for SK Hynix Fund
item.description: CSOP Asset Management Ltd. is lifting a ceiling on the use of options in a $14.4 billion leveraged exchange-traded fund linked to South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix Inc., giving the fund manager more flexibility after a wild rally in the company’s shares.

item.title: Chinese Oil Imports May Never Fully Recover From Iran War
item.description: Chinese oil imports may never fully recover from the Iran war, with analysts saying the conflict accelerated a permanent shift away from fuels like gasoline and diesel.

item.title: South Korea’s Policy Chief Flags Property Risks From Chip Windfall
item.description: South Korea’s presidential policy chief said policymakers need to consider how gains from the country’s chip-led boom will spread through the broader economy, warning that excess liquidity has historically found its way into the property market.
